response.justification: Wei Chen is an accomplished full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture experience, but the role's primary requirement is deep C++ expertise (8–12 years) for industrial automation platforms. The candidate's C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a student thesis project, which is wholly insufficient for a senior C++ architect role. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, MQTT or industrial protocol experience, or any cloud (Azure) background relevant to the position.
strengths: Solid event-driven and distributed systems background (high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec)
Node.js and React experience aligns with the role's listed advantageous skills
Demonstrated agile craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and mentoring/community contributions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis); the role requires 8–12 years of production C++ with STL and OOP depth
No industrial automation, IoT, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0)
No indication of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) knowledge, IEC 62443 standards, or industrial cybersecurity practices
